metadata.description: HRF evaluation with duration-based convolution mechanics and summate vs max modes

semantic_description.purpose: Validate complex HRF evaluation with duration-based convolution mechanics, temporal integration, and the critical distinction between summate vs max evaluation modes for block designs
semantic_description.algorithm:
The evaluate() method for HRF objects supports both point evaluation and duration-based block evaluation:
**Point Evaluation (duration < precision):** - Simple function evaluation: amplitude * hrf(grid) - Direct mapping from time points to HRF values - No temporal integration required
**Block Evaluation (duration ≥ precision):** 1. Create temporal offsets: offs = seq(0, duration, by=precision) 2. Evaluate HRF at shifted time points for each offset: hlist = [hrf(grid - o) for o in offs] 3. Apply combination rule based on summate parameter: - summate=TRUE: Sum responses (Reduce("+", hlist)) - summate=FALSE: Take maximum response (apply(hmat, 1, max))
**Multi-basis HRF Handling:** - Single-basis: Returns vectors, combined into matrix for duration processing - Multi-basis: Returns matrices, combined element-wise - summate=FALSE with multi-basis: Forces summate=TRUE with warning
**Critical Parameters:** - amplitude: Scaling factor applied to all evaluations - duration: Block width triggering temporal integration - precision: Temporal resolution for duration sampling - summate: Integration mode (sum vs max within duration window) - normalize: Post-processing peak normalization
semantic_description.mathematical_properties:
**Temporal Integration Properties:** - Point evaluation: h(t) = amplitude * HRF(t) - Block evaluation (summate): h(t) = amplitude * Σ HRF(t - δ) for δ ∈ [0, duration] - Block evaluation (max): h(t) = amplitude * max(HRF(t - δ)) for δ ∈ [0, duration]
**Summation vs Maximum:** - Summate=TRUE: Models accumulating neural activity over duration - Summate=FALSE: Models peak response within duration window - Different biological interpretations and mathematical properties
**Amplitude Scaling:** - Applied before duration processing: amplitude * HRF(t) - Preserves linearity for summate=TRUE - Affects peak detection for summate=FALSE
**Normalization (post-processing):** - Single-basis: out / max(abs(out)) - Multi-basis: column-wise normalization by peak absolute value - Applied after all other processing steps
semantic_description.edge_cases: - duration < precision: Should use point evaluation - duration = 0: Should use point evaluation - Very large duration: Should handle many temporal offsets - amplitude = 0: Should return zeros - negative amplitude: Should handle signed scaling - Multi-basis with summate=FALSE: Should warn and use summate=TRUE - Empty grid: Should handle gracefully - Single time point: Should work with duration

input.name: fine_time_grid
input.description: Fine time grid for precision testing

input.name: duration_values
input.description: Duration values for block evaluation testing

check.location: length(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5)))
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = 1, duration = 0)
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = 2, duration = 0)
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = -1, duration = 0)
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = 0, duration = 0)
max(abs(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 0.05, precision = 0.1) - evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 0)))
max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 3, summate = TRUE))
which.max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 3, summate = TRUE))
max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 3, summate = FALSE))
max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 5, summate = TRUE)) / max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 5, summate = FALSE))
abs(max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 2, precision = 0.1, summate = TRUE)) - max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 2, precision = 0.2, summate = TRUE)))
max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 6, summate = TRUE)) / max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 3, summate = TRUE))
ncol(evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 0))
nrow(evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 0))
ncol(evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 2, summate = TRUE))
max(abs(evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), amplitude = 2, duration = 0) - 2 * evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), amplitude = 1, duration = 0)))
max(abs(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), amplitude = 3.7, normalize = TRUE)))
max(apply(abs(evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), amplitude = 2.5, normalize = TRUE)), 2, max))
length(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, duration = 3, summate = TRUE))
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, duration = 3, summate = TRUE)
max(abs(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 0.01, precision = 0.1) - evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 0)))
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 0, duration = 5, summate = TRUE)
max(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(5, 15, by = 1), duration = 10, summate = TRUE))
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, duration = 1, precision = 0.5, summate = TRUE)
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = 2, duration = 3, summate = TRUE) / evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = 1, duration = 3, summate = TRUE)
max(evaluate(HRF_GAMMA, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 4, summate = TRUE))
max(evaluate(HRF_GAUSSIAN, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 3, summate = TRUE))
max(abs(evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5), duration = 0) - evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))))
evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, duration = 2, summate = FALSE)

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Function: evaluate.HRF(x, grid, amplitude=1, duration=0, precision=.2, summate=TRUE, normalize=FALSE) # Location: R/hrf.R lines 1020-1086
# Use built-in HRF objects - these are already defined globally # HRF_SPMG1 is built-in single basis # HRF_SPMG3 is built-in 3-basis with canonical + temporal + dispersion derivatives # HRF_GAMMA is built-in gamma HRF # HRF_GAUSSIAN is built-in gaussian HRF
# Test basic point evaluation time_grid <- seq(0, 20, by = 0.5) point_eval <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid)
# Test amplitude scaling amp_scaled <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = 2, duration = 0) amp_negative <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = -1, duration = 0) amp_zero <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, amplitude = 0, duration = 0)
# Test duration boundary duration_tiny <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 0.05, precision = 0.1) duration_zero <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 0)
# Test block evaluation modes block_summate <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 3, summate = TRUE) block_max <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 3, summate = FALSE)
# Test precision effects block_fine <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 2, precision = 0.1, summate = TRUE) block_coarse <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 2, precision = 0.2, summate = TRUE)
# Test duration scaling block_short <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 3, summate = TRUE) block_long <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, duration = 6, summate = TRUE)
# Test multi-basis HRFs multi_point <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, time_grid, duration = 0) multi_block <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, time_grid, duration = 2, summate = TRUE) multi_scaled <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, time_grid, amplitude = 2, duration = 0)
# Test normalization norm_single <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, time_grid, amplitude = 3.7, normalize = TRUE) norm_multi <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG3, time_grid, amplitude = 2.5, normalize = TRUE)
# Test single time point single_point <- evaluate(HRF_SPMG1, 6, duration = 3, summate = TRUE)
# Test other HRF types gamma_block <- evaluate(HRF_GAMMA, time_grid, duration = 4, summate = TRUE) gaussian_block <- evaluate(HRF_GAUSSIAN, time_grid, duration = 3, summate = TRUE)
implementations.Python:
# Python implementation following semantic specification
import numpy as np
class HRF: """Simple HRF class for evaluation testing.""" def __init__(self, func, name="hrf", nbasis=1, span=24): self.func = func self.name = name self.nbasis = nbasis self.span = span
def __call__(self, t): return self.func(t)
def evaluate_hrf(hrf, grid, amplitude=1, duration=0, precision=0.2, summate=True, normalize=False): """ Evaluate HRF with duration-based convolution mechanics.
Args: hrf: HRF object or function grid: Time points for evaluation amplitude: Scaling factor duration: Block duration (0 for point evaluation) precision: Temporal resolution for block evaluation summate: If True, sum over duration; if False, take max normalize: If True, normalize to peak absolute value of 1
Returns: Array or matrix of HRF values """ grid = np.asarray(grid, dtype=float)
# Base function with amplitude scaling def base_func(g): return amplitude * hrf(g)
# Evaluate based on duration if duration < precision: # Point evaluation out = base_func(grid) else: # Block evaluation offsets = np.arange(0, duration + precision/2, precision)
# Evaluate HRF at shifted time points for each offset hrf_values = [] for offset in offsets: values = base_func(grid - offset) hrf_values.append(values)
# Check if multi-basis (matrix output) is_multi_basis = len(hrf_values[0].shape) > 1 and hrf_values[0].shape[1] > 1
if is_multi_basis: # Multi-basis: always use summation if not summate: import warnings warnings.warn("summate=False is not typically used with multi-basis HRFs. Using summation.") out = np.sum(hrf_values, axis=0) else: # Single basis: stack into matrix and apply combination rule hrf_matrix = np.column_stack(hrf_values) if summate: out = np.sum(hrf_matrix, axis=1) else: out = np.max(hrf_matrix, axis=1)
# Apply normalization if requested if normalize: if len(out.shape) > 1: # Matrix case peaks = np.max(np.abs(out), axis=0) peaks[peaks == 0] = 1 out = out / peaks else: # Vector case peak_val = np.max(np.abs(out)) if peak_val != 0: out = out / peak_val
return out
